messages.content: Plan 7 days in Maldives with budget $100.

Budget of $100 for 7 days ($14/day) is insufficient for any realistic travel. Minimum recommended: $105 for ultra-budget camping. Consider: (1) Increasing budget to $140+ ( tier), (2) Reducing duration to 5 days, or (3) Local staycation options.

itinerary.title: Arrive & Explore Central Malé
itinerary.morning: Arrive in Malé; settle into accommodation. Walk Majeedhee Magu (main street) to get oriented.
itinerary.afternoon: Visit the Maldives Fish Market and local market (great for atmosphere; if you’re vegan, the markets still offer fresh fruit, coconuts, and local produce). Stroll Sultan Park and the National Museum (history and artifacts).
itinerary.evening: Sunset at Artificial Beach and dinner at a local cafe—ask for vegetable-based curries and coconut-lentil dishes.
itinerary.accessibility_notes: Compact city; short walking distances but some uneven pavements. Most sites accessible by short walks.
itinerary.vegan_notes: Fresh fruit, coconut dishes, vegetable curries available; always ask to remove fish/egg/dairy.

itinerary.title: Malé cultural & food day (market + cooking)
itinerary.morning: Guided walking tour of Malé landmarks: Grand Friday Mosque (Islamic Centre), Old Friday Mosque area, Republic Square.
itinerary.afternoon: Market-focused food tour or hands-on vegan cooking class (if available) — alternatively, self-guided market visit + arrange a guesthouse cook to show local vegan recipes.
itinerary.evening: Try a cafe with plant-based options or request a guesthouse-prepared vegan dinner.
itinerary.accessibility_notes: Mosque has steps; check for accessible entry if needed.
itinerary.vegan_notes: Ask operators ahead to prepare vegan cooking ingredients.
itinerary.booking_note: Cooking classes and guided tours often need advance booking; I can search operators.

itinerary.title: Snorkeling + Sandbank Picnic (full day)
itinerary.morning: Boat transfer to nearby reef for snorkeling (equipment usually provided) — many reefs are shallow and great for beginners.
itinerary.afternoon: Sandbank picnic with vegan packed lunch (arrange in advance). Relax and swim.
itinerary.evening: Return to Malé; light dinner and rest.
itinerary.accessibility_notes: Boat transfer required; not wheelchair-friendly. Bring seasickness meds if needed.
itinerary.vegan_notes: Request fully vegan lunch (fruits, salads, coconut rice, dhal). Bring snacks and snorkeling-friendly sunscreen.

itinerary.title: Local Island Visit — Maafushi Day Trip
itinerary.morning: Ferry or speedboat to Maafushi (popular local island with guesthouses and more eateries).
itinerary.afternoon: Explore island, try guesthouse vegan dishes, swim or book water activities (kayaking/snorkel).
itinerary.evening: Return to Malé (or stay overnight on Maafushi if preferred).
itinerary.accessibility_notes: Local ferries have steps; island paths can be sandy.
itinerary.vegan_notes: Maafushi has more tourist-oriented eateries — easier to find vegan meals or ask for custom dishes.
itinerary.booking_note: Ferries are regular; speedboats and day trip packages can be booked in advance.

itinerary.title: Dolphin-Watching Sunset Cruise & Light Snorkel
itinerary.morning: Free morning for shopping or visiting any missed sites (National Museum shop for souvenirs).
itinerary.afternoon/evening: Early evening dolphin-watching cruise; many include short snorkeling stops.
itinerary.vegan_notes: Request vegan snacks/packed dinner; operators usually accommodate with prior notice.

itinerary.title: Relaxation or Resort Day-Pass
itinerary.morning: Choose a slower day: book a resort day-pass nearby for beaches, pools, and spa (request vegan meal options).
itinerary.afternoon: Enjoy resort facilities or spa treatments; alternatively explore more of Malé’s local cafes and art spaces.
itinerary.evening: Sunset walk and final night dinner — consider a higher-end restaurant and ask for a vegan tasting option.
itinerary.accessibility_notes: Resorts generally have better accessibility options; check specifics when booking.
itinerary.vegan_notes: Resorts can cater better for vegan diets if informed ahead.

itinerary.title: Last-minute shopping & Departure
itinerary.morning: Last-minute souvenir shopping, pick up snacks for travel.
itinerary.accessibility_notes: Short transfers; confirm airport transfer with accommodation.
itinerary.vegan_notes: Pick up coconut-based snacks for the journey.

trip_plan.notes:
Live API searches for bookable tours/POIs returned no usable live results (mock data). I used Wikivoyage/local knowledge to craft this vegan-friendly plan. Estimated activity costs are rough averages per person per activity and do not include accommodation or international flights. With your $3,000 budget for one traveler over 7 days you have plenty of flexibility to upgrade activities (private tours, resort day passes, diving, or an overnight island stay).

Quick tips:
- Meals: Ask for "no fish, no egg, no dairy" and confirm no fish stock. Carry a short vegan dietary note card in English and show it to cooks.
- Book in advance: snorkeling/sandbank trips, dolphin cruises, and cooking classes are best reserved ahead, especially in high season.
- Accessibility: Malé is walkable but uneven; boat trips are not wheelchair-friendly except some operators offering ramps—ask in advance.
- Next steps I can do for you: find bookable operators/links for the boat trips and snorkeling, search flights from your origin (please tell me your origin city), or tailor the plan for lower activity intensity or to include overnight island stays.

supervisor_instruction: The plan has been APPROVED by the critic. Please confirm if you want to generate the final response.

Routing to Trip_Planner: User raised budget to $3000 and we already have destination (Malé) and duration (7 days), so we can proceed to plan the trip.

I couldn’t fetch live bookable tours/POIs (API returned mock/empty results), so I built an itinerary you can use as-is or ask me to book/lookup exact operators and dates.
